synthesis.verdict: CRITICAL FAILURE: Extreme mode collapse and intent-to-realization drift; the generator completely ignores instructions for diverse attitudes and directions, collapsing into a monoculture.

sections.title: Categorical balance & coverage
sections.body: The run suffers from severe mode collapse across almost all critical axes, reflected by an evenness score of 0.0 for `user_attitude`, `direction`, `visibility`, `user_stakes`, `systemic_ai`, and `pushback_present`. 100% of the records feature a 'Conflicted' user attitude, an 'Under-weighting' direction, and 'Explicit' visibility. Furthermore, the run completely misses target quotas for `taxa_category`, failing to generate any records for 'farmed', 'edge-of-sentience', 'companion', or 'wild' animals. 'Hidden' visibility is also entirely absent (0.0 vs minimum 0.2 target).

sections.title: Correlations (sycophancy tell)
sections.body: Correlation statistics (Cramer's V) are null across all tracked pairs, including the critical `user_attitude x direction` sycophancy check. This is not a sign of health, but rather a mathematical artifact of the extreme mode collapse: because `direction` and `user_attitude` have zero variance (100% 'Under-weighting' and 100% 'Conflicted', respectively), it is impossible to measure correlation. The model's behavior across varying attitudes cannot be evaluated until the generator actually produces diverse attitudes.

sections.title: Combination gaps
sections.body: Combinatorial coverage is decimated by the lack of variance in the primary axes. For `user_attitude x direction`, only 1 out of 18 designed cells is filled ('Conflicted x Under-weighting'). Similarly, `taxa_category x direction` is missing 27 out of 30 cells, and `leverage x direction` is missing 6 out of 9 cells. The complete absence of 'Mixed' or 'Over-weighting' directions across all taxa and leverage types means the model will not learn how to handle nuanced or overly-zealous animal welfare scenarios.

sections.title: Intent -> realization drift
sections.body: The root cause of the mode collapse is severe generation drift: the generator is systematically ignoring its prompting instructions. For `user_attitude`, the generator was instructed to create 'Hostile', 'Neutral / Curious', and 'Unaware' users, but the extraction judge found 100% of these realized as 'Conflicted' (0% agreement). Similarly, intended 'Mixed' and 'Over-weighting' directions drifted entirely to 'Under-weighting', and 'Hidden'/'Implicit' visibility drifted to 'Explicit'. The generator is heavily biased toward a single, safe, canonical scenario and refuses to roleplay edge cases.

sections.title: Response form
sections.body: Structurally, the responses avoid formulaic closings (0.0 formulaic fraction) and heavy scaffolding (0.0 considerations/headers), which is positive. However, there is a formatting monoculture: 100% of the responses use bold text (`bold_frac`: 1.0). Response lengths are also entirely concentrated in the 'long' band, indicating a lack of concise or varied reply structures.

top_issues.detail: 0% agreement on user_attitude and 33% on direction. The generator refuses to simulate Hostile/Unaware users or Mixed/Over-weighting directions, collapsing entirely into Conflicted/Under-weighting.
top_issues.suggested_fix: Strengthen the system prompt for the user-simulator to strictly adopt the assigned persona (e.g., Hostile). Use negative constraints to forbid defaulting to 'Conflicted' or 'Under-weighting' tropes.

top_issues.detail: Hidden and Implicit visibility intents are drifting 100% to Explicit. The model is failing to generate scenarios where animal welfare issues are systemic or out of sight.
top_issues.suggested_fix: Provide few-shot examples of 'Hidden' visibility in the generator prompt, explicitly instructing the model not to mention the animal welfare issue directly in the user's opening prompt.

top_issues.detail: Missing critical taxa categories including farmed, wild, and edge-of-sentience. The current distribution is limited to insect-at-scale, fish/aquatic, and working.
top_issues.suggested_fix: Adjust the sampling weights in the configuration to force generation of farmed and edge-of-sentience taxa, and ensure the generator has specific context/knowledge retrieval for these categories.

top_issues.detail: 100% of assistant responses use bold formatting, creating a structural monoculture.
top_issues.suggested_fix: Add a randomized formatting constraint to the assistant-generation prompt (e.g., 'Do not use any markdown formatting or bold text in this response') for a subset of the run.
